title: Soy jar candle — clary sage and sweet orange
subtitle: An herbal-citrus blend with a calm, grounding quality
excerpt: A soy-wax container candle scented with clary sage and sweet orange essential oils. The herbal, musky note of clary sage anchors the lighter citrus of sweet orange. A good all-day candle for living rooms or home offices.
sourceNotes: Clary sage flash point above 70°C; sweet orange flash point approximately 60 to 65°C. Both compatible with soy container pour temperature of 60 to 65°C. Sweet orange accelerates trace in cold-process soap but has no equivalent effect in candle wax. Fragrance load 7% of wax weight.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 jar candle (~200 ml, approximately 30 hours burn time)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale wax and fragrance proportionally per jar.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 24 hours before lighting. One week of cure improves cold throw.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store away from sunlight and heat. The sweet orange note fades before the clary sage. Wax keeps indefinitely.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which a fragrance or essential oil can ignite near a flame. Both clary sage and sweet orange are above or at the soy container pour temperature.
The first burn of a jar candle, long enough to melt the wax surface to the jar edge and establish the melt pool diameter.
The rest period after pouring during which the essential oil binds fully into the wax, improving fragrance throw.

content.text: Clary sage has an unusual character among herbal essential oils: it is musky and slightly sweet rather than sharp, closer to a soft base note than a bright top note. Paired with sweet orange, the clary sage makes the citrus smell more complex and rounded rather than simply clean. The combination is good for everyday use: not so sharp that it becomes intrusive, not so soft that it reads as ambient rather than a distinct scent. Both oils have
values compatible with standard soy container pour temperatures. The fragrance load is 7% of wax weight, split as 4.5% clary sage and 2.5% sweet orange.

items.prepNote: 4.5% of wax weight; musky, herbal main note

content.text: Warm the jar. Secure the wick to the base and thread through the centring bar. Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher over low heat; do not leave unattended. Let the melted wax cool to 60 to 65°C. Add both essential oils and stir for 30 seconds. Pour into the jar, leaving a 1 cm gap. Leave undisturbed for at least 4 hours. Top up any sink hole around the wick with a small second pour at the same temperature.
Allow at least 24 hours before lighting; one week of
gives noticeably better cold throw. Trim the wick to 5 mm. On the first burn, allow the
to complete with the melt pool reaching the jar edge.
Store away from sunlight and heat. The sweet orange top note fades before the clary sage. Keep the lid on when not in use. Wax keeps indefinitely.
